vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:claude-opus-4.8
vBRIEFInfo.description: Bugfix vBRIEF: replace the invalid 'satellite' SF Symbol in the GPS status view with the valid 'location.fill'

plan.title: Fix invalid GPS status SF Symbol

narratives.Overview: GPSSignalStatusView renders its icon with Image(systemName: "satellite"), but there is no SF Symbol named 'satellite'. At runtime the symbol fails to resolve, logging 'No symbol named satellite found in system symbol set' and rendering a blank glyph.
narratives.Problem: The GPS indicator shows no icon and the Xcode console is spammed with symbol-not-found warnings.
narratives.Goals: 1. Use a valid SF Symbol so the GPS indicator renders an icon. 2. Eliminate the console warning.
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: The GPS status view MUST use a valid SF Symbol that resolves on the deployment target.
FR-2: The console warning about the missing 'satellite' symbol MUST no longer appear.
narratives.Architecture: Single-symbol change in GPSSignalStatusView: replace 'satellite' with 'location.fill'.
narratives.TestingStrategy: Build succeeds and the existing test suite stays green; the symbol fix is verified by build + on-device/console observation (no console warning, icon visible).

items.title: Use location.fill in the GPS status view

narrative.Acceptance: GPSSignalStatusView uses Image(systemName: "location.fill"); the icon renders and no symbol-not-found warning is logged.
